016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.http.header;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.function.*;
024
025/**
026 * Represents an HTTP <c>Forwarded</c> header.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * Disclose original information of a client connecting to a web server through an HTTP proxy.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
033 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
034 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
035 * (and possibly earlier).
036 *
037 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
038 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
039 * </ul>
040 *
041 * @since 9.2.1
042 */
043public class Forwarded extends HttpStringHeader {
044
045   /** The header name */
046   public static final String NAME = "Forwarded";
047
048   /**
049    * Constructor with an eager value.
050    *
051    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
052    */
053   public Forwarded(String value) {
054      super(NAME, value);
055   }
056
057   /**
058    * Constructor with a lazy value supplier.
059    *
060    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
061    */
062   public Forwarded(Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
063      super(NAME, valueSupplier);
064   }
065
066   /**
067    * Static factory method with an eager value.
068    *
069    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
070    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
071    */
072   public static Forwarded of(String value) {
073      return new Forwarded(value);
074   }
075
076   /**
077    * Static factory method with a lazy value supplier.
078    *
079    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
080    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
081    */
082   public static Forwarded of(Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
083      return new Forwarded(valueSupplier);
084   }
085
086   /**
087    * Creates a new empty {@link Builder}.
088    *
089    * @return A new builder. Never <jk>null</jk>.
090    */
091   public static Builder create() {
092      return new Builder();
093   }
094
095   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
096   // Inner types
097   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
098
099   /**
100    * Fluent builder for assembling an HTTP <c>Forwarded</c> header value.
101    *
102    * <p>
103    * Composes the forwarding information defined by
104    * <a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7239'>RFC 7239</a> into the
105    * <c>key=value;key=value, key=value</c> wire format: <c>;</c>-separated parameters within a single forwarded
106    * element (one proxy hop) and <c>,</c>-separated elements across hops.  Typed setters cover the four registered
107    * parameters (<c>by</c>, <c>for</c>, <c>host</c>, <c>proto</c>); {@link #param(String, String)} is a generic escape
108    * hatch.  Values are emitted as bare tokens when possible and quoted automatically when they are not valid tokens
109    * (e.g. IPv6 node identifiers such as <c>"[2001:db8::1]:8080"</c>).  Call {@link #next()} to start a new hop.
110    *
111    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
112    * <p class='bjava'>
113    *    String <jv>value</jv> = Forwarded.<jsm>create</jsm>()
114    *       .forValue(<js>"192.0.2.60"</js>)
115    *       .proto(<js>"http"</js>)
116    *       .by(<js>"203.0.113.43"</js>)
117    *       .next()
118    *       .forValue(<js>"198.51.100.17"</js>)
119    *       .build();
120    *    <jc>// =&gt; "for=192.0.2.60;proto=http;by=203.0.113.43, for=198.51.100.17"</jc>
121    * </p>
122    *
123    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
124    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Forwarded}
125    *    <li class='extlink'><a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7239'>RFC 7239 - Forwarded HTTP Extension</a>
126    * </ul>
127    *
128    * @since 10.0.0
129    */
130   public static class Builder {
131
132      private final List<Map<String,String>> elements = new ArrayList<>();
133      private Map<String,String> current;
134
135      /**
136       * Constructor.
137       */
138      public Builder() {
139         current = new LinkedHashMap<>();
140         elements.add(current);
141      }
142
143      /**
144       * Sets the <c>by</c> parameter (the interface where the request came in to the proxy) on the current element.
145       *
146       * @param value The node identifier. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
147       * @return This object.
148       */
149      public Builder by(String value) {
150         return param("by", value);
151      }
152
153      /**
154       * Sets the <c>for</c> parameter (the client that initiated the request) on the current element.
155       *
156       * @param value The node identifier. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
157       * @return This object.
158       */
159      public Builder forValue(String value) {
160         return param("for", value);
161      }
162
163      /**
164       * Sets the <c>host</c> parameter (the original <c>Host</c> request header) on the current element.
165       *
166       * @param value The host. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
167       * @return This object.
168       */
169      public Builder host(String value) {
170         return param("host", value);
171      }
172
173      /**
174       * Sets the <c>proto</c> parameter (the protocol used to make the request) on the current element.
175       *
176       * @param value The protocol (e.g. <js>"http"</js>, <js>"https"</js>). Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
177       * @return This object.
178       */
179      public Builder proto(String value) {
180         return param("proto", value);
181      }
182
183      /**
184       * Generic escape hatch for setting any forwarded parameter by name on the current element.
185       *
186       * <p>
187       * Replaces any previously-registered value for the same parameter while preserving its original position.  The
188       * value is emitted as a bare token when valid, otherwise as an escaped quoted-string.
189       *
190       * @param name The parameter name. Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
191       * @param value The parameter value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
192       * @return This object.
193       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code name} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
194       */
195      public Builder param(String name, String value) {
196         assertArgNotNull("name", name);
197         assertArgNotNull("value", value);
198         var n = name.trim();
199         if (n.isEmpty())
200            throw iaex("forwarded parameter name must not be blank");
201         current.put(n, value);
202         return this;
203      }
204
205      /**
206       * Commits the current forwarded element and begins a new one (the next proxy hop).
207       *
208       * @return This object.
209       */
210      public Builder next() {
211         current = new LinkedHashMap<>();
212         elements.add(current);
213         return this;
214      }
215
216      private static boolean isToken(String value) {
217         if (value.isEmpty())
218            return false;
219         for (var i = 0; i < value.length(); i++) {
220            var c = value.charAt(i);
221            var ok = (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z') || (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z') || (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
222               || "!#$%&'*+-.^_`|~".indexOf(c) >= 0;
223            if (! ok)
224               return false;
225         }
226         return true;
227      }
228
229      private static String render(String value) {
230         if (isToken(value))
231            return value;
232         var sb = new StringBuilder(value.length() + 2);
233         sb.append('"');
234         for (var i = 0; i < value.length(); i++) {
235            var c = value.charAt(i);
236            if (c == '\\' || c == '"')
237               sb.append('\\');
238            sb.append(c);
239         }
240         sb.append('"');
241         return sb.toString();
242      }
243
244      /**
245       * Builds the rendered <c>Forwarded</c> header value.
246       *
247       * <p>
248       * Empty elements (hops with no parameters) are omitted.  Returns an empty string when nothing has been
249       * registered.
250       *
251       * @return The header value. Never <jk>null</jk>.
252       */
253      public String build() {
254         var rendered = new ArrayList<String>(elements.size());
255         for (var e : elements) {
256            if (e.isEmpty())
257               continue;
258            var parts = new ArrayList<String>(e.size());
259            e.forEach((k, v) -> parts.add(k + "=" + render(v)));
260            rendered.add(String.join(";", parts));
261         }
262         return String.join(", ", rendered);
263      }
264
265      /**
266       * Builds a {@link Forwarded} header bean directly from this builder.
267       *
268       * @return A {@link Forwarded} carrying {@link #build()}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
269       */
270      public Forwarded toHeader() {
271         return Forwarded.of(build());
272      }
273
274      @Override
275      public String toString() {
276         return build();
277      }
278   }
279}
